scores.dimension_name: Net Zero commitment and transition plan
scores.evidence_quote: committed to achieving Net Zero greenhouse gas emissions across our value chain by 2045
scores.evidence_location: p.3, Section 1 Net Zero Commitment and Transition Plan
scores.scoring_rationale: The report gives a Net Zero target year of 2045, a 2019 baseline, interim 2030 targets for Scope 1, 2 and material Scope 3, and states that progress is reported annually. It also outlines transition levers and committed decarbonisation capex, supporting a score of 3.

scores.evidence_quote: We disclose both location-based and market-based Scope 2 emissions
scores.evidence_location: p.4, Section 2 Scope 1 and Scope 2 Emissions
scores.scoring_rationale: The report states GHG Protocol reporting, operational control boundary, factors used, and discloses both location-based and market-based Scope 2. It provides 2019, 2022, 2023 and 2024 data showing an absolute market-based Scope 1 and 2 reduction trend, supporting a score of 3.

scores.evidence_quote: 7 are reported as material to Sentinel Records
scores.scoring_rationale: The report discloses all categories deemed material under a materiality assessment and explains why other categories are excluded. Supplier engagement is evidenced, but the report does not demonstrate that the supplier programme is already reducing Scope 3 emissions; Scope 3 is stated as behind trajectory, so score 2 rather than 3.

scores.evidence_quote: subject to limited assurance by Brackenbury Verification Services Ltd
scores.scoring_rationale: The report identifies an external assurance provider and states limited assurance under ISAE 3000 and ISAE 3410 for Scope 1, Scope 2 and selected Scope 3 categories. It does not claim reasonable assurance or coverage of all material Scope 3, so score 2.

The report explicitly states that near-term targets were validated by SBTi and shows targets covering Scope 1, 2 and material Scope 3. Long-term Net Zero validation is still under review, so this does not meet the score 3 threshold.

overall_notes: Disclosure quality is generally strong, with quantified emissions data, a clear 2019 baseline, interim targets, transition levers and external limited assurance. The main conservative scoring constraints are that Scope 3 supplier engagement has not yet evidenced actual emissions reductions, and long-term Net Zero target validation by SBTi is still under review. Renewable electricity is REGO-backed, which is weaker than direct PPAs or on-site generation, but this does not materially change the Scope 1 and 2 disclosure score.
